use: Point an AI or IDE at this file, or paste taste-card.md. Favor the principles; match the recurring tags; treat any 'rejection' entries as anti-patterns.

principles.title: Square icon buttons as the default
principles.why: Standardize button shapes/sizes to reduce visual clutter and improve hierarchy; square icon buttons chosen as the default for uniformity.

principles.title: 3-tier token architecture (primitive to semantic to component)
principles.why: Automate styling and keep themes aligned; primitive tokens are temporary scaffolding while transitioning to semantic defaults. Taste = systematized, not hand-tuned per screen.

principles.title: On-canvas animation as feedback, not decoration
principles.why: Animate the specific node that changed and zoom to it when AI adds something. Motion earns its place only when it communicates what happened.

principles.title: Mobile = clean view-only + AI chat, not a full editor
principles.why: Two journeys: create-quickly-to-expose vs. see-quickly (Figma/Miro view mode). Don't over-build mobile editing; prioritize viewing + AI chat.

principles.title: Reduce surface area, remove redundant UI
principles.why: Remove obsolete controls; demote destructive/rare actions into menus. Fewer, clearer affordances over more.

principles.title: Familiar mental models over novelty
principles.why: Reach for patterns users already know (VS Code, Figma view mode, Miro, Claude memory) so the interface feels learnable rather than clever.

entries: See the full manifest for all 18 entries with tags, dimensions, rationale, origin and image. This published file carries the summary + principles; the complete entry list lives in the Design Taste library manifest.
